Pandowdy Apple IIe Emulator
Table of Contents

Interface ICardFactory

Namespace
Pandowdy.EmuCore.Interfaces
Assembly
Pandowdy.EmuCore.dll

Factory for creating instances of peripheral cards from registered prototypes.

public interface ICardFactory

Examples

Typical usage in dependency injection: 

// Register card prototypes
services.AddSingleton<ICardFactory>(sp => new CardFactory(
    new ICard []
    {
        new NullCard(),
        new DiskIIController(),
        new SuperSerialCard(),
        new ThundercardController()
    }
));

// Use factory to install cards
var factory = serviceProvider.GetRequiredService<ICardFactory>();
var slots = new Slots(factory, rom, floatingBus, status);

slots.InstallCard("Disk II Controller", SlotNumber.Slot6);
slots.InstallCard(2, SlotNumber.Slot2); // By ID

Remarks

The ICardFactory manages a registry of card prototypes and provides methods to create independent instances of cards for installation in expansion slots. This design allows multiple slots to use the same card type without sharing state.

Prototype Pattern:
The factory maintains a single prototype instance of each card type. When a card is requested, the factory calls the prototype's Clone() method to create a fresh, independent instance. This ensures that:

  • Each slot gets its own card instance with independent state
  • Multiple slots can use the same card type simultaneously
  • Removing a card from one slot doesn't affect other slots
  • Card initialization occurs once during factory setup

Registration and Validation:
Cards are registered during factory construction (typically via dependency injection). The factory validates that:

  • No two cards share the same ID (Id)
  • No two cards share the same name (Name, case-insensitive)
  • A NullCard (ID = 0) is always registered

Registration violations throw InvalidOperationException during construction, preventing the emulator from starting with an invalid card configuration.

Retrieval Methods:
The factory provides three ways to retrieve cards:

  1. GetCardWithId(int) - Look up by numeric ID (fastest, used for serialization)
  2. GetCardWithName(string) - Look up by display name (user-friendly, used for configuration)
  3. GetNullCard() - Convenience method for empty slot placeholder (always succeeds)

All methods return null if the requested card is not registered, allowing callers to handle missing cards gracefully.

Thread Safety:
Implementations should be thread-safe for read operations (card creation). Write operations (card registration) occur only during factory construction. The prototype instances should not be modified after registration.

Methods

GetAllCardTypes()

Retrieves a list of all registered card types with their IDs and names.

List<(int Id, string Name)> GetAllCardTypes()

Returns

List<(int, string)>

A list of tuples containing (ID, Name) pairs for all registered cards, sorted by ID. Always includes at least the NullCard (0, "Empty Slot").

Examples

// Display all available card types
var cardTypes = factory.GetAllCardTypes();
Console.WriteLine("Available cards:");
foreach (var (id, name) in cardTypes)
{
    Console.WriteLine($"  [{id}] {name}");
}
// Output:
//   [0] Empty Slot
//   [1] Disk II Controller
//   [2] Super Serial Card
//   [3] Thundercard Controller

// Populate a UI dropdown
cardListBox.ItemsSource = factory.GetAllCardTypes()
    .Where(card => card.Item1 != 0) // Exclude NullCard
    .Select(card => card.Item2);

Remarks

This method provides a catalog of all available card types, useful for:

  • Populating UI card selection dropdowns/lists
  • Generating configuration documentation
  • Debugging and diagnostic tools
  • Validating configuration files against available cards

Ordering:
The list is sorted by card ID in ascending order, placing NullCard (ID = 0) first, followed by actual peripheral cards in ID order.

List Content:
Each tuple contains:

  • Item1 (int): The card's unique ID from Id
  • Item2 (string): The card's display name from Name

The returned list is a new collection that can be freely modified without affecting the factory's internal state.

See Also
GetCardWithId(int)
GetCardWithName(string)

GetCardWithId(int)

Creates a new instance of a card by its numeric identifier.

ICard? GetCardWithId(int internalId)

Parameters

internalId int

The unique numeric ID of the card type (see Id). Common values: 0 = NullCard, 1 = Disk II, 2 = Serial Card, etc.

Returns

ICard

A new independent instance of the requested card type via Clone(), or null if no card with the specified ID is registered.

Examples

// Install a Disk II controller (ID = 1) in slot 6
slots.InstallCard(1, SlotNumber.Slot6);

// Check if a card type is available
ICard? serialCard = factory.GetCardWithId(2);
if (serialCard != null)
{
    // Serial card is available
}

Remarks

This method is the fastest retrieval option since it uses direct numeric comparison. It's ideal for:

  • Deserializing saved configurations (IDs are stable)
  • Programmatic card installation where IDs are known
  • Performance-critical card lookups

Return Value:
The method returns null if the ID is not found, allowing callers to detect and handle missing cards. The InstallCard(int, SlotNumber) method converts this null into an InvalidOperationException.

Each call creates a new, independent instance via Clone(), ensuring that slots don't share card state.

See Also
GetCardWithName(string)
GetNullCard()

GetCardWithName(string)

Creates a new instance of a card by its human-readable name.

ICard? GetCardWithName(string name)

Parameters

name string

The display name of the card type (see Name). Comparison is case-insensitive. Examples: "Disk II Controller", "Super Serial Card".

Returns

ICard

A new independent instance of the requested card type via Clone(), or null if no card with the specified name is registered.

Examples

// Install a Disk II controller by name
slots.InstallCard("Disk II Controller", SlotNumber.Slot6);

// Case-insensitive lookup works
slots.InstallCard("disk ii controller", SlotNumber.Slot5); // Also works

// Check if a card type is available
ICard? serialCard = factory.GetCardWithName("Super Serial Card");
if (serialCard != null)
{
    // Serial card is available
}

Remarks

This method provides a user-friendly way to create cards using their display names instead of numeric IDs. The name comparison is case-insensitive to accommodate user input and configuration files. It's ideal for:

  • User configuration interfaces (card selection dialogs)
  • Text-based configuration files (JSON, XML, INI)
  • Command-line arguments and scripts

Return Value:
The method returns null if the name is not found (or doesn't match any registered card after case-insensitive comparison). The InstallCard(string, SlotNumber) method converts this null into an InvalidOperationException.

Each call creates a new, independent instance via Clone(), ensuring that slots don't share card state.

Name Uniqueness:
The factory validates during construction that no two cards share the same name (case-insensitive). This ensures that name-based lookup is unambiguous.

See Also
GetCardWithId(int)
GetNullCard()

GetNullCard()

Retrieves a new instance of the NullCard placeholder.

ICard? GetNullCard()

Returns

ICard

A new NullCard instance, or null if NullCard is not registered (which indicates a critical factory configuration error).

Remarks

This is a convenience method equivalent to GetCardWithId(0). It provides a clear, semantic way to obtain the empty slot placeholder without hard-coding the NullCard ID.

Critical Requirement:
This method must never return null in a properly configured emulator. The NullCard is required for:

  • Initializing all slots during Slots construction
  • Replacing removed cards via RemoveCard(SlotNumber)
  • Providing a safe fallback when card creation fails

Both Slots constructor and RemoveCard(SlotNumber) throw InvalidOperationException if this method returns null.

See Also
GetCardWithId(int)
NullCard

See Also

ICard
ISlots
NullCard
